issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1202: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the function after the guard clause.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function adds CSS classes and locks scroll but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will continue to announce the menu as collapsed even though it is visually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1858: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_lockScroll();` and before the `emit` call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus trap when drawer is open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation acts as a modal drawer (locks scroll, overlays content) but does not trap keyboard focus. Users can tab out of the drawer into the background page, making navigation difficult for keyboard and screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1859: Insert `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` after setting aria-expanded. Line 1895: Insert `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }` at the start of the close function before unlocking scroll.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the function animates the panel and emits events but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the item's trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3305: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` and before `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel and emits an event but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2103: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` and before `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField does not set aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation (size or type), the function announces the error via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the file input. Screen readers will not indicate that the field contains an invalid value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4212: Insert `binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the `if (!validation.valid)` block before the return statement. Additionally, clear the attribute in `_removeEntry` when the last invalid file is removed: Line 4235: Insert `if (binding.entries.length === 0) binding.inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not announce open/close state changes
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens or closes, no live-region announcement is made. Although focus is moved to the first item on open, users do not hear confirmation that the menu state changed, especially when closing via Escape where focus returns to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2108 (end of _openInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`. Line 2145 (end of _closeInstance): Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the mobileNav module adds the is-open class and locks scroll but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now expanded, while the close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false".

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in the dropdownMenu module removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The paired _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the faqAccordion module sets the item state to open and animates the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", leaving screen reader users unaware that the answer is expanded.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. This prevents assistive technology from identifying the field as invalid, affecting all forms using inlineValidator (newsletter, contact, wizard).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable but never initializes it with createFocusTrap in the init function, nor activates it in the open function. Keyboard focus can escape the mobile navigation drawer, violating WCAG 2.1.2 No Trap.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _onAccept function does not announce status change

issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid(): After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions fail to update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller updates the panel visibility and CSS classes but omits updating the triggering button's aria-expanded state. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance(): Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance(): Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the change in result count and visible items is not announced to screen reader users. Users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters(): Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` (or use appropriate string from STRINGS)

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced via the live region system. Although aria-expanded is updated, users do not receive confirmation of the action or the content that became visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line faqAccordion.open(): Before the closing brace, add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`); }`
  Line faqAccordion.close(): Before the closing brace, add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`); }`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users may not be aware that prices have updated or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod(): After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have an aria-controls attribute linking it to the navigation drawer element. Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init(): After `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (from Issue 6 fix), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid from valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared/validated successfully, the function removes the error description from aria-describedby but leaves aria-invalid="true" set, causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid(): After the if block that handles aria-describedby, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons toggle active states visually via CSS classes but do not update aria-pressed (or aria-selected) to reflect the pressed state to screen reader users. This fails WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter._syncTabAriaStates(): Inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the mobileNav module adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the menu state has changed to open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1645: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module removes the open class but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the menu state has changed to closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1670: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module hides the navigation drawer but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1690: if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not set aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module removes the visible class but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element, leaving it in the accessibility tree while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not set initial aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not announce state change to screen readers

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick function does not announce scroll action to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _onClick function in the scrollToTop module scrolls the page to the top but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users of the position change.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, markInvalid calls addDescribedBy to link the error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen reader users cannot identify which fields contain invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line in markInvalid(): add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded when opening/closing panels
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions manage the visibility of dropdown panels by removing or adding the 'hidden' attribute and CSS classes, but they fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _openInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line in _closeInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, there is no announcement via a live region. Screen reader users may not realize the dropdown has appeared or disappeared, especially if focus management does not clearly indicate the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _openInstance(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` Line in _closeInstance(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded when opening items
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function sets item.open = true and adds CSS classes, but does not set the aria-expanded attribute to "true" on the item.trigger element. While the close() function sets aria-expanded="false", the open function omits the corresponding true state, leaving screen reader users unaware that the accordion item has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users may not realize the content has changed unless they manually check the trigger button state.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title));` Line in close(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have updated or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line in setPeriod(): add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" and aria-controls pointing to the listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" when the suggestion listbox is closed. The attribute is also not updated when the listbox opens or closes. WCAG requires aria-expanded to indicate the popup state.
issuesFound.fix: Line after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`: add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` Update this to 'true' in suggestionListRenderer.show() and 'false' in suggestionListRenderer.hide().

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates in the DOM but this change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not know that filtering has completed or how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches): add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${totalMatches} products found\`);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation success does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, markValid removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line in markValid(): add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` after clearing aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as toggle buttons indicating the active category, but only CSS classes are toggled. They lack aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line inside forEach in _syncTabAriaStates: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not trap focus
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background content, making it difficult to navigate the menu and potentially accessing content that should be inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): add focus trap initialization: `if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); } _trap.activate();` and corresponding cleanup in close().

issuesFound.title: Search clear button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button is selected from the DOM using SELECTORS.searchClear, but the JavaScript does not ensure the button has an accessible name (aria-label). If the HTML lacks text content or aria-label, the button will be announced as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line after selecting _clearBtn: add `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_clearBtn.textContent.trim()) setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard navigation buttons missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next buttons control the visibility of step panels but lack aria-controls attributes linking them to the panel element IDs. This helps users understand which element they are controlling.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _updateNavButtons: add `if (_prevBtn && _current > 0) setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', _steps[_current - 1].panelEl.id);` and `if (_nextBtn && _current < _steps.length - 1) setAttr(_nextBtn, 'aria-controls', _steps[_current + 1].panelEl.id);`

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle buttons (data-theme-toggle) function as toggle buttons but do not update aria-pressed to indicate the active theme state.
issuesFound.fix: Line in click handler after setting theme: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` for the active button and `setAttr(otherBtns, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` for others.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function sets up event listeners but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the _trigger element. The initial state of the menu is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1505: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on inst.trigger. Users relying on screen readers will not know the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1633: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on inst.trigger. Users relying on screen readers will not know the dropdown is closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: siteSearch does not update aria-expanded on combobox

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not move focus to drawer when opened
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not programmatically moved to the drawer content. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button and must tab through the rest of the page to reach the navigation links. Additionally, the focus trap is declared but never activated.
issuesFound.fix: Line (open function): After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`, add focus management: `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` and activate the focus trap: `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded at page load

issuesFound.fix: Line (register function): After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch does not initialize aria-expanded at page load

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not announce state change

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when in error state, but no element with this ID exists in the DOM because FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: NewsletterForm component, FormGroup

      label="Email address"
      htmlFor="newsletter-email"
      errorId="newsletter-email-error"
      error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
    >

Line: ContactForm component, name FormGroup

      label="Full name"
      htmlFor="contact-name"

    >

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
